target.field_a: Non-equilibrium statistical mechanics — Mpemba effect spectral theory
target.field_c: Neurodegenerative protein biochemistry — amyloid aggregation selectivity

bridge_concepts: Mpemba index as eigenmode overlap integral of protein conformational ensemble with slowest MSM eigenmode
Spectral gap of combined folding/misfolding transition matrix as aggregation kinetics predictor
Non-normal Liouvillian dynamics creating transient misfolding acceleration zones
Rough energy landscape diffusion coefficient ratio D_misfold vs D_fold
Comparative Mpemba index across amyloidogenic vs non-amyloidogenic protein pairs

checks.name: KEGG Pathway Cross-Check
checks.query: APP (hsa:351) in neurodegeneration KEGG pathways; amyloidogenic protein pathway representation
checks.details: APP (hsa:351) confirmed in hsa05010 (Alzheimer disease) and hsa05022 (Pathways of neurodegeneration — multi-disease). hsa05010 contains >200 genes including PSEN1, BACE1, APOE, APP. hsa05020 (Prion disease) also confirmed. KEGG search for 'amyloid' returns APCS, APBB1/2/3, APBA1, SAA1/2/4. Full pathway infrastructure for amyloidogenic biology is computationally grounded. Note: KEGG does not encode Mpemba physics concepts — pathway check validates the biological substrate, not the cross-field bridge (which is assessed via PubMed co-occurrence).
checks.raw_evidence: curl rest.kegg.jp/link/pathway/hsa:351 → path:hsa05010, path:hsa04517, path:hsa04726, path:hsa05022

checks.name: STRING Interaction Verification
checks.query: Network for SNCA, APP, MAPT, TARDBP, FUS (human, species=9606)
checks.verdict: VERIFIED (>0.7 for all core amyloidogenic pairs)
checks.details: All five key amyloidogenic proteins are confirmed in high-confidence curated interaction networks. All are subjects of active MD simulation and MSM studies. Published MSMs confirmed for Aβ42, α-synuclein (multiple papers 2021–2026), tau. TDP-43 and FUS have emerging simulation literature.

checks.name: PubMed Co-occurrence: Mpemba AND amyloid
checks.details: Zero papers. Perfect disjunction confirmed. The Mpemba effect spectral framework has never been applied to amyloid aggregation. This confirms genuine novelty of the bridge.

checks.name: PubMed Co-occurrence: Mpemba AND aggregation
checks.title: Effect of initial temperature on water aggregation at a cold surface
checks.verdict: NOT RELATED — water aggregation, not protein. Bridge remains disjoint.
checks.details: The single hit concerns water molecular clustering on cold surfaces — unrelated to protein aggregation kinetics.

checks.name: PubMed Co-occurrence: Mpemba AND Markov state model
checks.title: Nonequilibrium thermodynamics of the Markovian Mpemba effect and its inverse
checks.verdict: CRITICAL FOUNDATION — mathematical framework exists; not applied to proteins
checks.details: This Klich & Raz (2017) paper establishes eigenmode-overlap formalism for the Mpemba effect in Markov chains (B_k = overlap of initial distribution with k-th left eigenvector). This is exactly the mathematical backbone of the proposed Mpemba index for protein MSMs. The protein application is absent. Generator must cite this as foundation and differentiate the protein MSM application as the novel contribution.

checks.name: PubMed Co-occurrence: eigenmode overlap AND Mpemba
checks.title: Quantum Mpemba Effect from Non-Normal Dynamics
checks.verdict: ACTIVE AREA — non-normal Liouvillian confirmed as distinct mechanism; no protein application
checks.details: 2025 paper directly formalizes non-normal dynamics as a distinct Mpemba mechanism separate from eigenmode overlap. Directly relevant to bridge concept #3 (non-normal Liouvillian transient acceleration). Active research area with no protein connection yet.

checks.name: PubMed Co-occurrence: Mpemba effect spectral theory (broader)
checks.pubmed_query: Mpemba effect AND (spectral OR eigenvalue OR relaxation)

key_papers.title: Analytical diagonalization of dynamical generator of reaction networks; strong Mpemba effect in Markov jump processes
Typical strong Mpemba effect and Liouvillian gap (PRL)
Mpemba effect driven by distinct metastable state trapping under energy barriers
Nava & Egger: Mpemba effects in arbitrary Markovian systems (PRL)

checks.verdict: MATHEMATICALLY MATURE — Mpemba spectral theory is an active, productive physics subfield
checks.details: The mathematical infrastructure for the hypothesis is well-developed and accelerating (2025-2026 publication surge). Multiple papers now treat energy-barrier-mediated Mpemba effects in Markov jump processes — mechanistically analogous to amyloid nucleation trapping.

checks.name: Biological Self-Assembly Precedent: Mpemba Shortcuts in Viral Capsid Assembly
checks.title: Nonequilibrium Hasty Shortcuts in Self-Assembly
checks.significance: CRITICAL BRIDGING PAPER — validates Mpemba spectral framework (eigenmode overlap suppression) in biomolecular self-assembly (viral capsid). Direct precedent for amyloid application. Same mathematical formalism (B_k overlap integrals, Markov chain eigendecomposition). Confirms the approach extends beyond thermal quench systems to biologically relevant assembly processes.
checks.verdict: PARTIAL PRECEDENT CONFIRMED — closest existing biological analog to the proposed amyloid Mpemba application
checks.advisory: Generator must cite PMID 37606329 as the biological self-assembly precedent. Differentiate: capsid paper optimizes protocol to accelerate assembly; proposed hypothesis predicts that natural variation in initial conformational ensemble produces Mpemba-like selectivity between amyloidogenic and non-amyloidogenic sequences.

checks.name: MSM Data Availability for Amyloidogenic Proteins

alpha-synuclein.examples: JPCB 2021 (membrane interactions MSM); ACS Chem Neurosci 2022 (misfolding dimer MSM); PMID 41225715 (2025, alpha-syn/14-3-3 assembly MSM with rugged landscape)

Abeta42.examples: Piana et al., Lindorff-Larsen et al. (structure ensemble models); implied timescales reported

tau.examples: ScienceDirect 2020 (conformational dynamics); tau repeat domain models

TDP-43.examples: Active simulation area; aggregation literature emerging

FUS.examples: Phase separation literature; few MSM papers

cross_alpha_amyloid_msm.title: MSMs from 50 microseconds of MD elucidate cross-alpha amyloid relaxation
cross_alpha_amyloid_msm.significance: Directly validates MSM approach for amyloid aggregation kinetics — proof of concept for the computational methodology

checks.verdict: VERIFIED for monomer MSMs; aggregation MSMs emerging
checks.limitation: Existing published MSMs are primarily monomer-level. Combined folding+misfolding MSM with amyloid as absorbing state requires new construction (feasible but not off-the-shelf).

checks.name: Quantitative Plausibility: D_misfold / D_fold ratio
checks.claim: D_misfold approximately 1000x slower than D_fold on energy landscape

python_calculation.formula: D_fold/D_misfold = exp(ε_misfold² - ε_fold²) in kT units

experimental_support.pmid_41894766: Feng et al. 2026 PRL: directly measures D at protein folding barriers; D increases with protein length due to cooperative contact formation reducing roughness
experimental_support.pmid_38189619: Li et al. 2024: quantifies energy landscape roughness via random position-dependent friction; few to few kcal/mol range confirmed
experimental_support.pmid_30793144: Chu & Wang 2019 PCCP: position-dependent D in IDP binding-coupled folding; D reflects roughness

checks.verdict: PLAUSIBLE — 1000x is conservative; within published roughness range; could be 10⁴–10⁷x
checks.advisory: Present as 'order-of-magnitude or greater' rather than exact 1000x. Frame as a testable prediction (novel measurement) rather than established fact.
checks.warning: No published paper has directly measured D_fold vs D_misfold for the same amyloidogenic protein. This is a novel experimental prediction.

checks.name: Mpemba Spectral Conditions: 3-State Model Analysis
checks.critical_caveat: Simple 3-state kinetics gives EXPECTED result (stressed = 10x faster early aggregation). The TRUE Mpemba analog requires the native state to have LOWER slow-eigenmode overlap — causing anomalously fast aggregation from the seemingly more ordered state. This requires specific eigenmode computation from actual protein MSMs, not simple kinetic arguments.
checks.verdict: MECHANISTICALLY COHERENT with caveats — requires eigenmode computation to confirm or refute

mpemba_conditions_satisfied.non_trivial_spectral_gap: YES (121,000x in model; 2-4 orders of magnitude in published MSMs)
mpemba_conditions_satisfied.non_normal_transition_matrix: YES for irreversible aggregation (detailed balance broken)

checks.name: Mathematical Consistency: Mpemba index B_k formula

components.psi_k: k-th left eigenvector of transition matrix T

checks.verification: For row-stochastic T: stationary distribution π is left eigenvector for λ=1 (unique for ergodic chains). B₂ (slowest relaxation mode overlap) is well-defined for any p₀. Mpemba effect occurs when |B₂(A)| < |B₂(B)| despite p₀_A further from π. This is exactly the Klich-Raz formalism (PNAS 2017, 2019). Formula is mathematically valid and tractable.

checks.name: Novelty Assessment: Mpemba index applied to amyloid MSMs
checks.details: Zero papers apply Mpemba effect spectral theory to protein aggregation or amyloid formation. Both component fields are independently productive and mathematically mature. The cross-field bridge is unexplored. This is consistent with a high-quality discovery target.

computational_readiness.foundation_paper: PNAS 2017, PMID 28461467 — Klich & Raz, Markovian Mpemba effect eigenmode overlap formalism
computational_readiness.quantitative_claims_status: PLAUSIBLE (D ratio: within measured roughness range; spectral gap: confirmed in MSMs)

key_concerns: PNAS 2017 provides the core mathematical framework — Generator must cite it and clearly differentiate the novel protein MSM application
Aggregation MSMs require combined folding+misfolding transition matrix — not off-the-shelf; requires new construction from existing simulation data
D_misfold vs D_fold ratio is inferred from roughness measurements, not directly measured for amyloidogenic proteins — present as prediction
Mpemba condition is non-trivially different from simple kinetics — requires actual eigenmode computation from protein MSMs
Non-normal Liouvillian dynamics: applies to irreversible aggregation regimes; detailed balance holds at early reversible oligomerization stages
Cross-protein comparative Mpemba index requires consistent MSM construction methodology across different proteins
generator_advisories: Cite PNAS 2017 (PMID 28461467) as mathematical backbone; novelty is protein MSM application to amyloid selectivity
Cite Bera et al. 2026 (PMID 41879186) for Markov jump process Mpemba theory with energy barriers — closest to amyloid nucleation analogy
Use Aβ42 as primary test case — best published MSM data (Piana et al., Lindorff-Larsen et al.)

Primary falsifiable prediction: Mpemba index from Aβ42 monomer MSM predicts anomalously fast aggregation from specific initial conformational ensembles, testable via ThT fluorescence assay comparing native vs stressed Aβ42
Present D_misfold/D_fold as 'order-of-magnitude or greater' — not exact 1000x
Flag non-normal Liouvillian dynamics as exploratory/speculative — strongest for nucleation-committed states where aggregation is effectively irreversible
Leverage PMID 41665928 (2026 cross-alpha amyloid MSM) as proof-of-concept that aggregation MSMs are feasible
Mpemba index computation is tractable: sparse matrix eigensolver on existing MSMs (N~10³ states); full methodology is implementable
Cite PMID 37606329 (2023, hasty shortcuts in viral capsid self-assembly) as the closest biological self-assembly precedent — same eigenmode decomposition formalism applied to assembly Markov chains
PMID 39762250 (2025, trapped ion Mpemba) supports experimental feasibility of optimal initial-state engineering for Mpemba speedup
run_version: v2 (supplementary checks added: viral capsid precedent PMID 37606329, timescale agnosticism, kcal/mol D ratio cross-check)
